title: Midyear Review: From Spring Slump to Summer Surge | Dimensional
gist: Dimensional's H1 2026 midyear review: the S&P 500 hit records on AI-driven chipmaker strength (+10%, Nasdaq +14% to June 19), emerging markets led globally (+29%), and small-cap/value sharply reversed 2025 underperformance (Russell 2000 +20.7% vs Russell 1000 +10%; Russell 3000 Value +16.3% vs Growth +5.2%). A new Fed under Kevin Warsh held rates at 3.5-3.75% amid rising inflation, with markets now pricing a possible hike rather than a cut. Gold fell ~20% since the Feb 28 war in Iran, undercutting its safe-haven reputation.

themes.theme: US Equity Rally and AI/Tech Leadership

insights.claim: The S&P 500 rose 10% and the tech-heavy Nasdaq added 14% as of June 19, 2026, hitting record levels in late May/early June driven by chipmakers and AI-spend beneficiaries.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: Confirms AI capex remains the dominant tape driver into mid-2026; chipmaker momentum, not breadth, is carrying the index.
insights.quote: The S&P 500 rose 10% and the tech-heavy Nasdaq added 14% as of June 19.
insights.locator: The US market struck fresh highs

insights.claim: Technology rebounded after an earlier decline led by software stocks, with AI enthusiasm boosting chipmakers rather than software broadly.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: Intra-tech divergence — software lagged while semis led — signals investors are paying for AI infrastructure, not application-layer software, this cycle.
insights.quote: Technology companies rebounded from an earlier decline by software stocks, as AI enthusiasm continued to boost chipmakers
insights.locator: The US market struck fresh highs

insights.claim: SpaceX debuted in June 2026 via the largest IPO on record, raising $75 billion, amid a wave of expected large tech and AI-related offerings this year.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: A record-breaking IPO window signals risk appetite and supply of new AI/tech equity; watch for further AI-company listings absorbing capital.
insights.quote: Shares of SpaceX made their debut in June in what was the largest IPO on record
insights.locator: The US market struck fresh highs

themes.theme: Global Rotation: EM and International Leadership

insights.claim: The MSCI Emerging Markets Index (net dividends) gained 29% as of June 19, 2026, sharply outpacing the MSCI ACWI (+12%) and MSCI World ex USA (+10%).
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: EM is the standout 2026 trade so far; US-centric portfolios are materially underexposed to the leading region.
insights.quote: The MSCI Emerging Markets Index (net dividends) fared much better, gaining 29%.
insights.locator: Global equities, as measured by the MSCI

insights.claim: Over the past five years through June 2026, non-US developed market value stocks have quietly led among equity asset classes, a result that may surprise US-focused investors.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: Challenges the US-mega-cap-only narrative; international value has been the durable five-year winner, arguing for global diversification.
insights.quote: non-US developed market value stocks have quietly led among equity asset classes
insights.locator: Small and Value Start the Year Strong

insights.claim: Leadership shifts unpredictably across regions and styles, so maintaining broad global equity exposure beats trying to pick the next winner.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: Dimensional's core thesis: diversification captures rotating leadership rather than betting on persistence of recent winners.
insights.quote: it's difficult to predict which stocks, sectors, or regions will lead from one period to the next
insights.locator: The first half of 2026 has only reinforced

insights.claim: US small caps surged in H1 2026 with the Russell 2000 up 20.7% versus the Russell 1000 large-cap index up 10.0% as of June 19, a sharp reversal from 2025 underperformance.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: The size premium re-emerged strongly; small-cap-tilted portfolios are outperforming after a multi-year drought.
insights.quote: the Russell 2000 Index (small caps) gained 20.7%, while the Russell 1000 Index (large caps) added 10.0%

insights.claim: Value sharply beat growth in the US in H1 2026, with the Russell 3000 Value Index up 16.3% versus the Russell 3000 Growth Index up just 5.2% as of June 19.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: The value premium delivered a ~11-point spread over growth; large-cap growth was a notable laggard, supporting a value tilt.
insights.quote: The Russell 3000 Value Index (net dividends) added 16.3%, while the Russell 3000 Growth Index rose 5.2%

insights.claim: Low profitability stocks outpaced high profitability stocks in global developed markets in H1 2026 (Fama/French Developed Low Profitability +14.8% vs High +6.5%), while the reverse held in emerging markets (High +35.4% vs Low +5.4%).
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
entities: Fama/French Emerging Markets High Profitability Index
insights.so_what: The profitability factor inverted by region — a junk-rally in developed markets but quality leadership in EM — complicating a uniform factor stance.
insights.quote: high profitability stocks were outpaced by low profitability stocks in global developed markets, while the opposite was true in emerging markets
insights.locator: Small and Value Start the Year Strong

themes.theme: Fed, Inflation and Rates Under New Leadership

insights.claim: The Fed held the federal-funds target at 3.5%-3.75% at its June 2026 meeting, the first under new Chairman Kevin Warsh, amid headline inflation rising to its highest level since 2023.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: New Fed leadership is holding firm against re-accelerating inflation; rate-sensitive assets face a higher-for-longer regime.
insights.quote: It was the first meeting under new Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh.

insights.claim: Rate-cut expectations collapsed in 2026: CME FedWatch odds of a lower fed-funds rate after the December meeting were 96% in late February but markets now favor a rate increase before year-end.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: A near-complete repricing from cuts to a possible hike — a major macro regime shift driven by oil-fed inflation that reprices duration and rate-sensitive trades.
insights.quote: the probability the fed-funds rate would be lower than the prevailing rate after the Fed's December meeting was 96% in late February; the odds now favor a rate increase before the end of the year

insights.claim: Core CPI rose 2.9% year-over-year in May 2026 and stayed relatively steady, while the 10-year Treasury yield climbed to 4.46% as of June 19, near its highest in over a year.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: Sticky ~3% core inflation plus a 4.5% 10-year keeps real yields elevated and pressures long-duration bond prices.
insights.quote: the yield on the benchmark 10-year Treasury up to 4.46% as of June 19

themes.theme: Oil, Gold and Geopolitical Risk Reassessed

insights.claim: Gold fell nearly 20% since the onset of the war in Iran on February 28, 2026, erasing earlier-year gains, while the S&P 500 rose almost 10% over the same period.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: Directly undercuts the gold-as-geopolitical-hedge thesis; in this conflict gold lost while equities gained, challenging safe-haven allocations.
insights.quote: Since the onset of the war in Iran on February 28, an investment in gold has fallen by nearly 20%

insights.claim: Global equity returns have not been highly correlated with oil prices, and the US economy is less vulnerable to oil shocks now given its services orientation and more diversified oil sources.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: Argues against reflexively de-risking equities on oil spikes; oil is one of many factors, not a dominant equity driver.
insights.quote: the US economy may be less impacted by oil shocks now than at some times in the past, as the economy has become more services-oriented and oil sources are more diversified

insights.claim: Across 21 geopolitical events over 36 years, equity returns were slightly negative for the first five days but turned positive over subsequent horizons, consistent with markets repricing quickly on negative news.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: Historical base rate argues against selling equities into geopolitical shocks; the initial dip tends to recover, favoring staying invested.
insights.quote: Looking at 21 of these over 36 years, returns were slightly negative for the first five days, but then turned positive over subsequent horizons.
insights.locator: Global equity markets have remained near record highs

insights.claim: Gold has had negative years more frequently than the US stock market and lacks a clear basis for a positive expected return, unlike equities which compensate risk-taking.
insights.speaker: Dimensional Fund Advisors
insights.so_what: Dimensional's structural argument against gold allocations; note disclosed conflict that Dimensional does not trade physical gold.
insights.quote: it's challenging to support positive expected returns simply for holding gold
insights.locator: This reinforces the importance of evaluating
